title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission · Program Brief: Symlink Root Policy
summary: Recursive discovery follows a symlink outside content, includes hidden drafts and generated output, treats directories as documents, and overwrites two sources onto one route. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the symlink root policy increment in bounded content-discovery manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.
Declare roots, extensions, ignores, hidden files, drafts, ordering, maximum files, depth, and total bytes before traversal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-paths-content-discovery-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-paths-content-discovery-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_paths_content_discovery_4(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-symlink-root-policy-c6064d
# Competency: ssg-symlink-root-policy.
# Case 2638: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_symlink_root_policy_c6064d(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_c6064d = "c6064d-d4606c-2638"
    assert evidence_variant_c6064d
    evidence_axes_c6064d = (
        "readerc6064d",
        "source2638",
        "parsed4606c",
        "routec6064d2638",
        "artifact2638c6064d",
        "recoveryd4606c2638",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_c6064d)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Symlink Root Policy model · Theory Symlink Root Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Symlink Root Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 2679 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a bounded content manifest with explicit rejection, root, and collision evidence; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Symlink Root Policy model · Theory Symlink Root Policy.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Symlink Root Policy model · Theory Symlink Root Policy, keep Symlink Root Policy connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.
